solutions.solution: c. Anything which needs overhauling must be obsolete. Also, anything that is not improved it? So, both are implicit. The statement makes one comment about the quality of TV itself and the only talks about the quality of TV programmes but that does not say that these mementits are a good entertainment medium. So, assumption I is not implicit because it is a restricted statement.

a. Banking services meet business needs. This means banking is a part of business activity. So, I is implicit. If we allow for the assumption of "business needs" includes "industrial needs", assumption II is not implicit.

a. The statement tells us about an effect produced by Vitamin E. If it is a good effect we may call the effect desirable. If it is not, we may call the effect undesired. How do we know? Structurally, there is not much difference between "Vitamin E makes your complexion glowing" and "Snake venom makes your senses dull." How can we say if the mentioned effect of Vitamin E is positive? Here, the keyword is "improving your circulation". Improvement is positive thing, so the effect of improvement of circulation, i.e. a glowing complexion, must also be a positive thing. So, I is implicit. Hence, I is implicit. But II is not. If vitamin E is present, we can have improved circulation and hence good complexion. But this does not mean that he converses is also true, i.e., if we do not have an improved circulation, we have dull complexion. Further, if we take the large amount of the question in the absence of circulation." If circulation itself is absent, man will die! Then it is a question of life or having complexion, then!

c. Since an advertisement offers "best training" in the field of "computers" it must have been assumed by the advertisers that people want "computer" training and that they want the "best training". So, both are implicit.

b. The speaker says nothing about the inevitability or non-inevitability of delay. So, I is not implicit. However, since he statement talks of "more people should be made more people can will increase." - people being given delay", it must have assumed that some delay will come if it must have assumed some delay will increase.

a. The advertisement wants girls "able to speak fluently in English". So, I is implicit. Good height is not mentioned as a requirement for a good job. But we should not jump to the conclusion that height does not matter in modelling. Note that the statement wants presentable girls. It can be assumed that very short girls would not be as presentable as the taller ones. So, II is not implicit.

a. When I say that I did something madly, I mean that I was out of senses while doing that act but otherwise, I am not mad, I am fine. Mad men do not say that "I chased him like a mad man." And, more importantly, even when they say such a thing, they assume that they are sane. Only I is implicit.

a. If relying on Shaleem was foolish, it obviously follows that Shaleem is unreliable. Again, II is not implicit in this case for the same reasons as those in the previous question. Remember, an assumption is something assumed to be true. If I march ahead when, It makes a statement. When I say that "I did something foolishly", I actually want to say that I am wise. I know what doing something foolishly means, I can see the difference, and there I did a mistake and in that case I acted foolishly. Hence, I is not implicit.

b. Since taste "contributes" to the intake of food, one reason why people eat is the taste of food. But this is only one of possibly many more reasons. But the given assumption I is a too definitive. It makes it look as if taste is the only reason why people eat. The statement does not go so far in its support for taste. So, I is not implicit. Obviously, human beings must be experiencing food, otherwise there would have been no question of its enjoyment. Hence, II is also implicit.

b. If a notice is given, it must have been assumed that it will have its desired effect. Hence, II is implicit. But I is doubtful. We do not know that 8:30 a.m is the required arrival time. The notice might be for an urgent meeting scheduled at 8:30 am.

b. The advertisers must have assumed that they were well-equipped while declaring that they guarantee "success". But we cannot generalise this assumption. "Such classes are well-equipped" is a generalised statement which means that "all classes are well-equipped" and this may not be true. But II is obviously implicit.
